__doc__: Machine-readable view of what the test suite covers, at the level of product surfaces rather than individual files.
Each entry maps a single surface (a coherent claim the suite makes about the parser) to:
- fixture_dirs: where the supporting inputs live
- verifiers: which scripts own the claim
- baselines: which JSON/text baselines lock the current state
- policy: 'zero-tolerance' (any failure blocks) or 'baseline-gated' (regressions block, known gaps tracked)
- coverage_status: 'strong' | 'medium' | 'weak' — honest assessment of how well the surface is measured today
- notes: free-text explanation of the current status, why it's at that level, and what would lift it

surfaces.name: core_syntax
surfaces.description: Valid ES2015..ES2025 programs parse and emit the expected ESTree shape.
surfaces.notes: Positive fixtures parse clean with pinned golden outputs; spec_fixtures runs every hand-authored fixture through deep-diff vs OXC.

surfaces.name: invalid_syntax
surfaces.description: Malformed programs are rejected with at least one parse error.
surfaces.notes: Truncation, regex, and numeric-literal negative families present; parser-negative gate covers them all. Six numeric-literal separators are still silently accepted and tracked as known bugs.

surfaces.name: early_errors
surfaces.description: Module-vs-script and strict-mode semantic early errors.
surfaces.notes: Fixtures exist (module_context/ and strict_mode/) but no dedicated verifier owns this surface yet; these are parser-adjacent semantic claims and will need a separate gate to become visible.

surfaces.name: ambiguity_ts_jsx
surfaces.description: JS/TS/JSX boundary cases parse deterministically in the right grammar mode.
surfaces.notes: 10 focused fixtures. 3 pass both golden and deep compare; 7 are tracked as known_fail — one genuine divergence (004 generic-arrow recovery) and six mode-mismatch cases where run_tests.sh parses in tsx mode but verify_ambiguity parses per-fixture intent (ts/jsx/js), producing different but both-correct ASTs. Aligning the two gates on a single lang choice per fixture would lift most to pass.

surfaces.name: recovery
surfaces.description: Parser recovers from malformed input and preserves later anchor declarations.
surfaces.notes: Expression/statement/declaration/jsx-ts recovery families all present with anchor-after-error discipline; assertions cover crash, bounded errors, anchor survival, no Unknown blowups, no absurd spans.

surfaces.name: differential_estree
surfaces.description: Kessel's ESTree JSON matches OXC (and where normalisable, Acorn and Babel) on real and synthetic inputs.
surfaces.notes: Deep compare runs by family (ambiguity, asi, escapes, interactions, jsx, lexical, regex_disambiguation, typescript, unicode) and across three reference parsers on curated real-world files.

surfaces.name: estree_node_coverage
surfaces.description: Every emitted ESTree node type has at least one minimal fixture exercising its emit path.
surfaces.notes: 57 node types, one fixture per emit path. Structural invariants run across the 467-file real-world corpus.

surfaces.name: standards_pressure
surfaces.description: Curated Test262 subset passes or is tracked by grammar category.
surfaces.notes: 66 syntax-only tests curated across lexical/expressions/statements/functions/modules. Early-errors category is still empty; two negative string-literal fixtures are tracked as known bugs.

surfaces.name: interaction_combinations
surfaces.description: High-value feature combinations parse correctly end-to-end.
surfaces.notes: 10 stacked-feature fixtures parse cleanly (0 parse errors); 3/10 match OXC exactly today; 7/10 diverge and are baseline-tracked. Lifting this to medium requires closing the deep-diff gap on decorators, import attributes, JSX-async, and generic-TS classes.

surfaces.name: lexical_tokenization
surfaces.description: Tokenisation-sensitive cases (BOM, hashbang, identifier escapes, numeric separators, template raw/cooked, comment-regex boundary, unicode line terminators) produce the correct AST shape.
surfaces.notes: 8/10 shape assertions pass. Two tracked lexer gaps: (a) BOM-before-hashbang swallows the hashbang, (b) U+2028/U+2029 are not treated as LineTerminators at top level.
